Description: Decide whether this repo should ship EvalOps Copilot, Evaller, or Evaller as a sub-flow inside EvalOps. Record the decision and route implications.
User Value: Prevents wasted implementation on the wrong product.
Current State: Docs say EvalOps. Active UI says Evaller.
Gap / Risk: Continued drift across docs, UI, tests, and data model.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Review prompt.md, docs/PRODUCT.md, active routes, and README.md. Produce a short decision record that chooses the canonical product surface and lists exact routes/nav labels to keep or change.
Definition of Done: Decision record exists and ROADMAP.md reflects the chosen direction.
Acceptance Tests: Product owner can answer which routes should exist and what brand appears in metadata, nav, and landing copy.

Task Name: Repair Playwright route and copy contract
Description: Update app behavior or tests after the product decision so Playwright validates the intended app.
User Value: Restores CI trust and catches regressions.
Gap / Risk: Red CI blocks serious continued development.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Fix the Playwright E2E failures by aligning tests and app route behavior with the canonical product decision. Do not weaken assertions. Run npm run test:e2e and document any remaining failure.
Definition of Done: Browser E2E passes or remaining failures are explicitly blocked by external credentials.

Description: Make Dashboard, Projects, Trace Import, Eval Builder, Graders, Prompt Optimizer, Routing & Caching, Reports, and Settings usable as distinct screens if EvalOps remains the target.
User Value: Users can complete the advertised Eval Debt Audit workflow.
Current State: Broad UI exists in WorkspaceApp, but routes redirect to /workspace.
Gap / Risk: MVP pages appear to exist in build output but are not actually exposed as intended workflows.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Wire the required EvalOps MVP pages to the existing broad WorkspaceApp or refactor equivalent route components. Preserve active functionality and do not delete Evaller code.
Definition of Done: Each required route renders a meaningful screen with shared navigation and project context.
Acceptance Tests: Navigate to each required route and verify the expected page header and primary actions render.
Files Likely Affected: src/app/dashboard/page.tsx, src/app/projects/page.tsx, src/app/trace-import/page.tsx, src/app/eval-builder/page.tsx, src/app/graders/page.tsx, src/app/prompt-optimizer/page.tsx, src/app/routing-caching/page.tsx, src/app/reports/page.tsx, src/app/settings/page.tsx, src/components/workspace-app.tsx, src/lib/navigation.ts

Task Name: Rebuild project switcher and left nav
Description: Ensure authenticated navigation matches required MVP screens and includes a clear project switcher.
User Value: Users know where they are and can move through the audit.
Current State: Active nav has Workspace, Runs, Templates, Settings only. Legacy project selection exists in inactive UI.
Gap / Risk: Confusing route model and hidden project flows.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Update the authenticated app shell so it exposes the canonical MVP navigation and project switcher. Use existing styles and preserve mobile behavior.
Definition of Done: Nav and project switcher work on desktop and mobile.
Acceptance Tests: Playwright verifies route links, active states, and project selection persistence.

Task Name: Document and consolidate EvalOps vs Evaller models
Description: Decide whether Evaller ai_test tables are temporary, a subdomain, or the canonical model. Add migration notes.
User Value: Reduces data confusion before real customers.
Current State: Both models exist in code and migrations.
Gap / Risk: Duplicated concepts make exports, billing, RLS, and reports harder to reason about.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Audit EvalOps and Evaller tables/types, propose a target boundary, and update docs/DATA_MODEL.md with exact ownership and migration implications. Do not drop data.
Definition of Done: Data model boundary is documented and future migration tasks are identified.
Acceptance Tests: Type and migration audit shows no unknown core entity ownership.
Notes: No destructive migration in this task.

Task Name: Configure and run preview production smoke
Description: Set required preview env vars outside the repo and run the smoke script against a preview deployment.
User Value: Confirms real Supabase, OpenAI, Inngest, Storage, billing gates, and isolation work.
Current State: Smoke script exists but local audit failed due missing env vars.
Gap / Risk: Production path may be broken despite local deterministic tests.
Suggested Codex Prompt: With preview credentials configured outside the repo, run npm run smoke:production against the preview URL and record failures with exact root causes and fixes.
Definition of Done: Preview smoke passes or has a clear blocker list with owner and remediation.

Description: Confirm .env.example, readiness checks, and deployment docs include every required env and no secrets.
User Value: Operators can configure the app safely.
Gap / Risk: Drift can break production or leak sensitive config.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Re-audit process.env usage and readiness checks, then update .env.example and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md if any env var is missing or mislabeled.
Definition of Done: Env docs match code references and readiness behavior.
Acceptance Tests: rg process.env has matching .env.example entries for app-owned envs.

Task Name: Verify Supabase auth and route protection
Description: Test signup, login, redirects, session refresh, and protected routes with real Supabase preview config.
User Value: Customers can access only their workspace.
Current State: Auth code and tests exist. Live preview was not verified.
Gap / Risk: Auth regressions are high-risk for customer data.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Verify Supabase Auth against preview with real test users, including signup, login, route protection, API rejection, and organization selection. Add or update tests for gaps.
Definition of Done: Auth flow is verified and documented.
Acceptance Tests: Manual or automated preview auth test passes.

Description: Run CSV, JSON, NDJSON, and TXT imports through production-mode Supabase/Inngest/OpenAI processing.
User Value: Proves the core Eval Debt Audit input path works.
Current State: Parser, API, Inngest, and OpenAI boundaries exist.
Gap / Risk: Not verified live in this audit.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Exercise the trace import flow with all supported file types in preview and fix any API, storage, Inngest, or UI status issues found.
Definition of Done: Each supported file type imports and produces derived artifacts.
Acceptance Tests: Preview test uploads complete and show generated eval cases/report data.

Description: Confirm CSV eval pack, issues CSV, audit CSV/PDF, full project JSON, receipts, and download routes work with real storage.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Verify each export type in preview with a processed project and repair any storage, filename, receipt, or content issues.
Definition of Done: Export artifacts download and contain expected project data.
Acceptance Tests: Downloaded files open and match project records without raw secret leakage.

Description: Verify Supabase RLS and Storage isolation across two organizations/users.
Current State: RLS policies and smoke intent exist.
Gap / Risk: Remote status in old docs may be stale after newer migrations.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Run tenant isolation checks against the linked Supabase preview project for both EvalOps and Evaller tables plus private Storage buckets. Document any policy fixes.
Definition of Done: User A cannot read or download user B data.
Acceptance Tests: Automated or manual RLS tests pass for app API and direct Supabase client reads.

Description: Update app metadata, landing copy, nav labels, and docs to match the chosen product surface.
User Value: Reduces buyer confusion and supports credible onboarding.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Align product naming across layout metadata, landing, login/signup, app shell, legal pages, README, and tests based on the product decision.
Definition of Done: Product name and scope are consistent in visible UI and docs.
Acceptance Tests: Search for Evaller/EvalOps terms and confirm only intentional references remain.
Files Likely Affected: src/app/layout.tsx, src/components/landing-page.tsx, src/components/app-shell.tsx, src/app/login, src/app/signup, docs, README.md

Task Name: Audit state quality on active MVP routes
Description: Review all required MVP routes for meaningful empty, loading, error, retry, and disabled states.
User Value: App feels trustworthy with partial or failing data.
Current State: Some state handling exists in both UI layers.
Gap / Risk: Inactive routes hide real UX quality.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Audit the canonical MVP routes and add missing empty, loading, error, and retry states using existing primitives and copy tone.
Definition of Done: Each route handles no data, loading, API failure, and success states cleanly.
Acceptance Tests: Playwright or component tests cover at least one failure/empty state per critical workflow.

Description: Decide whether Stripe Checkout is used for private MVP or whether billing stays manual/invoiced.
User Value: Prevents accidental access or payment confusion.
Current State: Stripe paths and billing tables exist.
Gap / Risk: Commercial copy and billing model not fully approved.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Review Stripe code paths and commercial checklist, then document the private MVP billing decision and any code gates needed before launch.
Definition of Done: Billing posture is documented and UI does not imply unavailable billing.
Acceptance Tests: Billing routes behave according to chosen posture in test mode and preview.

Description: Break workspace-app.tsx and evaller-app.tsx into focused route/view components after product direction is stable.
User Value: Future changes become safer and easier.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Refactor one oversized component into smaller view/components without changing behavior. Add tests or snapshots where useful and run the full relevant suite.
Definition of Done: Behavior is unchanged and files have clearer ownership.
Acceptance Tests: Existing tests pass and manual smoke shows no UI regression.

Description: Turn raw trace purge logic into a scheduled and observable workflow.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Add a scheduled/operator-safe raw-retention purge path with logging, receipts, and documentation. Use existing privacy operations.
Definition of Done: Raw purge can run safely and report what changed.
Acceptance Tests: Test mode verifies retained raw traces are purged while derived artifacts remain.

Description: Add Sentry and/or PostHog only after privacy and event taxonomy are approved.
User Value: Operators can debug and understand usage.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Implement minimal privacy-safe error monitoring and analytics with documented event names and disabled raw content capture.
Definition of Done: Errors have correlation IDs in monitoring and analytics capture approved events only.
Acceptance Tests: Trigger test error and verify no raw trace content is captured.

Description: Verify primary workflows with keyboard and accessibility checks.
User Value: Makes the product usable and credible.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Audit the canonical MVP routes for labels, focus, keyboard flow, icon labels, color-only status, and mobile accessibility. Fix high-impact issues.
Definition of Done: Critical workflows pass keyboard smoke and automated a11y checks where tooling is available.
Acceptance Tests: Manual keyboard checklist passes and any automated a11y tests pass.

Description: Keep local command-centre CSV/JSON exports and optional webhook sync current after roadmap changes.
User Value: Portfolio sheet can track project status.
Current State: Fallback exports are created in this audit.
Suggested Codex Prompt: Update scripts/export-roadmap-to-command-centre.mjs so it reflects current ROADMAP.md rows, then run it. If COMMAND_CENTRE_WEBHOOK_URL is set, verify webhook sync.
Definition of Done: Command-centre files match roadmap rows and summary score.
Acceptance Tests: CSV/JSON validate against the required schema.
